index.filterLabel: Browse the public feed by type

featuredSection.title: Keep the clearest public item visible, even when publishing is light.
featuredSection.summary: A pinned surface can carry one current public event notice or opportunity without forcing the site into a busy newsroom pattern.

feedSection.title: Confirmed updates, opportunities, and event notices in one calm feed.
feedSection.summary: Recurring Saturday sessions stay on the Sessions route. This feed is for occasional events, public-facing updates, and wider opportunities.

emptyState.title: No public items are live right now.
emptyState.summary: Use the sessions and contact routes for live support questions while this editorial space waits for confirmed public dates, updates, or opportunities.

filterEmptyState.title: No items match this filter right now.
filterEmptyState.summary: Try another filter, or use the sessions and get involved routes for the clearest live next steps.

archiveNotice.title: Past event handling
archiveNotice.body: When dated event items expire, they should step down in emphasis or archive cleanly instead of staying pinned as if they are still upcoming.

categories.label: Events
categories.description: Occasional public dates, workshops, and community event notices.

categories.label: Updates
categories.description: Public-facing changes, notices, and direction-setting updates.

categories.label: Opportunities
categories.description: Ways to get involved, support the work, or take a practical next step.

items.title: Community event dates will appear here once they are confirmed.
items.summary: Occasional community events and workshops are part of the wider public offer, but dates are only published when they are ready to share.

intro.title: Community events and workshops
intro.summary: This page keeps occasional public event guidance in one place without pretending details are confirmed before they are ready.
intro.supportingText: Use the contact route if you want to ask whether a wider public event is being planned.

sections.title: Public event notices should be useful, not speculative.
paragraphs: EncouragingYou may occasionally share wider community events or workshops, but this route only publishes them when there is enough confirmed information to make a visit feel realistic.
That keeps the site honest at launch and stops the editorial feed from sounding busier than the real public rhythm.
bullets: One place for occasional event guidance

sections.title: The wider public offer may include community events and workshops.
paragraphs: The public location anchor remains Rochdale, but exact venue information should only appear when it is appropriate to publish.
Date and time details will be added here once they are ready to share publicly.

sections.title: Use the live or support routes for anything current.
paragraphs: If you are looking for immediate Saturday support, start on Sessions rather than waiting for an event notice.
If you need to ask whether something wider is coming up, use Contact so the team can reply directly.

proofNotice.title: Public detail boundary
proofNotice.body: This event page stays live as a trusted holding point, but it should not claim date, venue, or booking details until those facts are confirmed.

relatedSection.title: Use a current route while event details are still light.
relatedSection.summary: The editorial family should point people back into practical routes when that is the more useful next step.

ctaBand.title: Use contact or live sessions while wider public event details are still being confirmed.
ctaBand.summary: This page should stay available as a calm editorial reference, not a substitute for current support routes.

items.title: Recurring Saturday support stays on the Sessions route.
items.summary: Use the Sessions page for the live Saturday offers. This feed stays reserved for broader updates, occasional events, and public notices when they are ready to share.

intro.title: Live Saturday support stays on the Sessions route
intro.summary: This update keeps the site boundary clear: recurring CV support and Youth Club remain on Sessions, while Events & Updates stays lighter and more occasional.
intro.supportingText: If you need a current time, calendar file, or attendance guidance, start with Sessions rather than waiting for an editorial post.

sections.title: Recurring support no longer shares space with occasional editorial notices.
paragraphs: The Sessions route is now the public source of truth for live Saturday offers, practical detail, and calendar links.
Events & Updates stays reserved for occasional public notices so the site does not become a second timetable.

sections.title: Use Sessions when you need something current and actionable.
paragraphs: Choose Sessions if you want live timings, session detail, or the clearest route into current support.
Use Contact if you are unsure which Saturday offer fits or you need help before attending.

relatedSection.title: Other public updates around the site
relatedSection.summary: The editorial family can stay small while still pointing to adjacent public routes.

ctaBand.title: Go straight to Sessions when you want current support details.
ctaBand.summary: This update is here to reduce confusion, not to replace the live route itself.

items.title: Volunteer, partner, or refer someone through one clear route.
items.summary: If you want to help, collaborate, or point someone toward support, the Get Involved route is the current public starting point.

intro.title: Volunteer, partner, or refer someone through one clear route
intro.summary: This opportunity post points people to one current starting place for volunteering, partnership conversations, and referrals.
intro.supportingText: Use the dedicated hub when you want a practical next step without guessing which route applies.

sections.title: One route works better than scattering support, partner, and referral prompts.
paragraphs: The current public starting point is a single Get Involved route that separates motivations clearly once someone arrives.
That keeps volunteering, partnership, and referral conversations visible without making the editorial feed do the work of a hub page.

sections.title: The Get Involved route holds the practical detail.
paragraphs: Use Get Involved for route cards, privacy-aware next steps, and the clearest handoff into volunteer, partner, or referral journeys.
This opportunity entry stays short so the editorial layer can signal the route without trying to replace it.

proofNotice.title: Scope note
proofNotice.body: This opportunity post should direct people into the hub without inventing volunteer role specifics or named partnership arrangements that are not yet public.

relatedSection.title: Useful context before or after you visit the hub
relatedSection.summary: The editorial family can connect public opportunities to current support and event guidance without collapsing the routes together.

ctaBand.eyebrow: Ready to take the next step?
ctaBand.title: Start on the Get Involved hub, then use Contact if you need something more specific.
ctaBand.summary: The hub is the current public route for volunteering, partnership, referral, and wider supporter journeys.
